Off Calendar Nov-14-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-22-2011 Failure To Post Jury Fees

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-04-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 to place the Settlement Agreement of the parties on the record. Judge: Tomar Mason, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR #7235.

Plaintiff Steven Nemec was present in pro per, placed under oath examined.
James C. Bridgman, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Columba Canas, dba Building Solutions.
Judicial Arbitration Order was issued on June 20, 2011.
Request for Trial De Novo After Judicial Arbitration was filed on July 18, 2011.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, November 28, 2011.

Settlement agreement reached by the parties was placed on the record by Mr. Bridgman. In accordance with the terms of the agreement, defendant Columba Canas, dba Building Solutions, ("defendant") shall pay plaintiff the sum of $3,000.00 as follows: Commencing January 25, 2012, and continuing on the 25th of each and every month thereafter, defendant shall pay plaintiff Steven Nemec $500.00. Defendant's $500.00 monthly payments shall be postmarked no later than the 25th of each month. If defendant complies with the terms set forth above, plaintiff shall file a dismissal after the six month payment period concludes.

If defendant missses a monthly payments, she shall have thirty (30) days to cure said default. Notice of any missed payment shall be mailed to defendant's counsel, James C. Bridgman. If defendant fails to cure the default within the thirty (30) days, plaintiff Steven Nemec may, upon ex parte application, obtain judgment for the full balance of thirteen thousand five hundred ($13,500.00) dollars, minus any payments.

Each party shall bear their own attorney fees and costs. Additionally, if defendant complies with the terms of the settlement agreement, plaintiff shall not report this matter to the California Contractors' State License Board (CCSLB).

Agreement reached by the parties was adopted by the Court. Matter is CONTINUED to October 4, 2012 @ 10:00 a.m. in Department 610 on the CRC 3.1385 Dismissal Calendar.
